threat_description: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash
Email Collection: Remote Public or Shared Mailbox
Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control:
Endpoint Denial of Service: Application Exhaustion Flood:

Establish Accounts: Adversaries may create accounts with third-party provider (TPP) services to legally probe the Open Banking APIs for vulnerabilities. They might also create fake social media profil

Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Adversaries will place malware or scripts in registry run keys or user startup folders on Windows 11 workstations to ensure their code executes automatically upon lo

Modify Authentication Process: The stated gap that "MFA not universally enforced for internal applications" is a critical vulnerability. An attacker with standard user credentials can move laterally t

System Binary Proxy Execution: Adversaries will use legitimate, signed Microsoft binaries (e.g., rundll32.exe, regsvr32.exe) to execute malicious code. This helps bypass application whitelisting and c
Domain Trust Discovery: Adversaries will map out the AD domain and forest trusts to understand how they can move from the office LAN into more sensitive zones, such as the core banking network.
System Language Discovery: Especially relevant for a Nordic bank, attackers will check system language settings to tailor social engineering or avoid detection by appearing as a local user.

Archive Collected Data: Before exfiltration, adversaries will stage collected data on an internal server and compress it into encrypted archives (e.g., password-protected ZIP files) to avoid detection

Data from Network Shared Drive: Attackers will search SharePoint Online sites and on-premises file shares for sensitive documents, such as internal audits, network diagrams, M&A plans, or customer PII

Non-Standard Port: To evade simple port-based firewall rules, attackers may run their C2 protocol over a non-standard port that is commonly allowed outbound, such as port 53 (DNS) but using TCP instea

Financial Theft: For a sophisticated actor, the primary objective could be direct financial theft. By compromising the SWIFT secure zone and obtaining operator credentials, they could initiate fraudul

Inhibit System Recovery: As part of a ransomware attack, adversaries will actively target and delete backups that are accessible from the network before encrypting production systems, undermining Nord
